channel.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
channel.description:
Hey all,
I am attempting to retrieve the options page data for a theme i'm building. I followed the previous threads like:
https://community.frontity.org/t/acf-options-page/767
&
https://community.frontity.org/t/how-to-access-acf-options-pages/2075
but I am still finding no success.
I am attempting to hit this endpoint:
https://gatorworksfron.wpengine.com/wp-json/acf/v3/options/options
to just see the data BUT the problem i'm having is that the handler i'm making isn't added to the frontity.libraries.source and I don't know why.
Here is a link to clone my repo:
git clone https://brian_tucker@bitbucket.org/brian_tucker/gatorworks-frontity-theme.git
I just updated it and made it public. Any help would be greatly appreciated, thanks!

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: brian.tucker
item.description:
I was able to get it to work, I had to combine my beforeSSR functions and use a Promise.all on the fetch calls. I am able to see the options now though. Thank you @mburridge & @Johan for the help!

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: brian.tucker
item.description:
I was able to get the file to work with this, thanks! I’m still struggling to fetch the data from the endpoint though

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: Johan
item.description:
Making a handler isn’t that complex, although there’s a minor “feature” (aka issue) with patterns which look like regular URL’s.

The following should work (not tested):

export const acfHandler = {
    name: "acf-options",
    priority: 10,
    pattern: "@acf/options",
    func: async ({ link, params, state, libraries }) => {
      const { api } = libraries.source;
      const { slug } = params;
  
      // 1. fetch   the data you want from the endpoint page
      const response = await api.get({
        endpoint: `/acf/v3/options/options`,
      });
  
      // 2. get an array with each item in json format
      const items = await response.json();
  
      // 3. add data to source
      const currentPageData = state.source.data[link];
      Object.assign(currentPageData, {
        items: items,
        isAcfOptions: true,
      });
    },
  };
  
  export default acfHandler;

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: brian.tucker
item.description:
Would i be attempting to use that in the beforeSSR? I was able to view the acfOptionsHandler in frontity.libaries.source now but the data for the options is not be fetched. I tried using ‘libraries.source.api.get’ and then populating, but i found no success.

I followed your instructions on how to add the Wordpress menus and got a better understanding of handlers. But not able to replicate the addition of this handler using a seperate file, i get the error “cannot read property ‘startswith’ of undefined”

When keeping the handler inside the root index, it adds to the frontity.libaries.source fine. Really not sure why the data isn’t fetching.

Any other insight would appreciated, thank you for the help so far!

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
Hi @brian.tucker

Welcome to the Frontity community.

You may need to use libraries.source.api.get to fetch the data from a specific endpoint such as https://gatorworksfron.wpengine.com/wp-json/acf/v3/options/options.

You will then also need to add the fetched data into the state using libraries.source.populate, as libraries.source.api.get doesn’t automatically populate the state in the way that actions.source.fetch does.

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped

item.title: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
item.dc:creator: brian.tucker
item.description:
Hey all,

I am attempting to retrieve the options page data for a theme i’m building. I followed the previous threads like:

ACF Options page 🙋Get Help
Hi, I just can’t figure it out: how can I access the ACF options page and the data inside? I’m using the acf-to-rest-api plugin and the API endpoint is right there: /wp-json/acf/v3/options/options But I just don’t know how to use it inside mars-theme. Thank you!

&

How to access ACF Options pages 🙋Get Help
Hi heroes of Frontity, I started playing around with Frontity this week and am very excited about it! Now, I want to try and rebuild our corporate site with Frontity. We use ACF to expand WordPress. I started trying to access an options page called ‘theme-identity-settings’, but it just won’t work. I want to use the content of this endpoint on multiple pages, so I figured it should be added in the beforeSSR? I came across this topic, where it seems to work out for countfak. Either I’m messing…

but I am still finding no success.

I am attempting to hit this endpoint:

https://gatorworksfron.wpengine.com/wp-json/acf/v3/options/options

to just see the data BUT the problem i’m having is that the handler i’m making isn’t added to the frontity.libraries.source and I don’t know why.

Here is a link to clone my repo:

git clone Bitbucket

I just updated it and made it public. Any help would be greatly appreciated, thanks!

Read full topic
item.source: Need help retrieving options page data, previous forums haven't helped
